summary: Re-linked the Dolphin GX runtime library entry for `dolphin/gx/GXFifo.c` by changing it from `NonMatching` to `Matching` in `configure.py`. The only source edit changed a match-only dead-code guard in `GXSetCPUFifo` from `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` to `#ifndef BUGFIX`, keeping the required inline `nop` present for the normal matching build while making the condition align with the repository's bugfix-toggle convention.
what_changed: In `configure.py`, `Object(NonMatching, "dolphin/gx/GXFifo.c")` was changed to `Object(Matching, "dolphin/gx/GXFifo.c")` in the `RuntimeLib` object list for the `gx` library.
In `src/dolphin/gx/GXFifo.c`, a conditional around an inline `asm {nop}` in `GXSetCPUFifo` changed from `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` to `#ifndef BUGFIX`.
No PR body, review comments, or discussion were present in the provided slice; the rationale must be inferred from the diff and title.
smart_moves: The PR did the minimal relink needed: it only changed the object status in the build configuration once the file could be treated as matching.
The source-side guard preserved the known required dead-code `nop` for matching builds while avoiding the narrower `MUST_MATCH` conditional.
The existing comment was left intact, documenting that the `nop` appears to be dead code but is still required to match the function.
decomp_lessons: When a source file depends on artificial or dead-looking code for binary equivalence, guard it using the project's bugfix convention if the code should remain in normal matching builds and only disappear under bugfix builds.
A file should not be promoted from `NonMatching` to `Matching` in `configure.py` until the source conditionals used by the matching build actually emit the required instructions.
Do not delete suspicious dead code merely because it is unreachable or semantically unnecessary; for decompilation, emitted instruction shape can matter more than source-level behavior.
Small conditional-compilation changes can be enough to re-link an entire object as matching.
naming_conventions: Runtime library object status entries use `Object(Matching, "path/to/File.c")` and `Object(NonMatching, "path/to/File.c")` in `configure.py`.
The project uses `BUGFIX` as a conditional compilation knob for code that should differ when enabling bug-fixed behavior.
The previous `MUST_MATCH` guard appears to be less appropriate here because the required `nop` must exist for the standard matching configuration.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: A literal inline assembly `nop` inside `GXSetCPUFifo` is used to preserve binary matching despite comments noting it is apparently dead code.
Switching from `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` to `#ifndef BUGFIX` makes the `nop` part of the default matching build output.
The tactic is to encode required compiler/codegen artifacts directly but isolate them behind a build flag so bugfix-oriented builds can omit them.
follow_up_queries: Look for other PRs that replace `MUST_MATCH` with `#ifndef BUGFIX` around match-only assembly or dead code.
Audit remaining `dolphin/gx` `NonMatching` entries in `configure.py` for similar small guard or codegen issues.
Search for comments like `dead code` and `needed to match` to find other places where matching artifacts are intentionally preserved.
